data.title: Honda open to reviving merger talks if Nissan CEO exits - report
data.description:
Honda (NYSE:HMC) is ready to revive merger talks with Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) if the latter's CEO Makoto Uchida resigns, the Financial Times reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Merger talks between the Japanese automakers fell through after Honda (NYSE:HMC) proposed that Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) become a fully owned subsidiary, instead of the two firms being on equal footing. Honda made the pitch as it was dissatisfied with the pace of Nissan's turnaround efforts.

Honda (HMC) is prepared to restart negotiations with a new leader at Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) who can better manage internal opposition. Uchida intends to stay at the helm till 2026, but Nissan's board is said to have begun informal discussions over when he should step down.

Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) needs to finalize a deal soon to avert a looming cash crunch as sales continue to deteriorate. Foxconn (OTCPK:HNHAF) last week confirmed that it'd be open to acquiring Nissan shares as the world's largest contract manufacturer eyes diversifying into the electric vehicle sector. Foxconn has already approached Renault (OTCPK:RNSDF), which is looking to sell part of its 36% stake in Nissan.

However, the Japanese establishment seeks to ward off Foxconn (OTCPK:HNHAF) given its perceived closeness to China. Private equity firms like KKR (KKR) and U.S. tech companies have reportedly been asked to consider investing in Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY).

data.title: Vietnam plans to welcome Musk's Starlink amid trade tensions - report
data.description:
Vietnam plans to allow Elon Musk’s Starlink (STRLK) satellite internet service to operate within its borders, marking a significant development in its telecommunications landscape, Reuters reported, citing a regulations draft and people with knowledge of the matter.

It can be viewed as "an olive branch" to SpaceX amid nervousness in Vietnam about tariff threats from U.S. President Donald Trump, a person familiar with the matter said.

data.title: Samsung plans to cancel 3.05T won shares; nominates chip execs to board
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) plans to cancel 3.05T won (about $2.11B) worth of its own stock that it had acquired previously, according to a regulatory filing by the company.

The South Korean tech giant also said in a separate filing that it would buy 2.7T won worth of its common stock, and 304B won worth of other shares, between Feb. 19 and May 16, 2025, to raise shareholder value and benefit its employees, Reuters reported.

In November 2024, Samsung said it planned to repurchase shares worth 10T won over one-year after its stock price underperformed competitors such as compatriot SK hynix, marking the first time Samsung had decided to buy back stock since 2017, the report added.

Separately, Samsung has nominated its chip business chief Jun Young-hyun and Chief Technology Officer Song Jai-hyuk to join its board, Reuters reported citing a regulatory filing.

The company said in the filing that it had nominated Seoul National University Professor Lee Hyuk-jae as an outside director. Lee is head of Seoul National University's semiconductor research center, the report added.

With two chip executives and one semiconductor academic nominated to its board, the company aims to bolster its focus on chips at the top level of the organization, the report noted.

A shareholder meeting is scheduled for March 19, wherein the nominations would be voted upon.

Samsung did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The market for high-bandwidth memory, or HBM, is led by SK hynix, which is also a major supplier of these chips to Nvidia (NVDA). Samsung has been making efforts to regain its competitiveness in the chip business. American chipmaker Micron Technology (MU) also makes HBM chips.

Last month it was reported that Samsung's 8-layer HBM3E, less advanced than the newer 12-layer product, was approved by Nvidia in December 2024. These chips, reportedly, would be used for Nvidia's less powerful AI processors customized for the Chinese market,

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Baidu Q4 beats top-line and bottom-line estimates
Earnings Snapshot: iQIYI misses Q4 top-line estimates, membership services revenue drops 15%
HIVE Digital Technologies announces appointment of Craig Tavares as President and COO of Buzz HPC
data.description:
HIVE Digital Technologies (NASDAQ:HIVE) has announced the appointment of Craig Tavares as President and COO of Buzz HPC. In this role, Tavares will be responsible for leading the company's GPU Cloud and High-Performance Computing (HPC) data center business.

This announcement comes as part of HIVE's strategic focus on sustainable data center infrastructure.

Tavares, with 20-year of experience in digital infrastructure expert, has a strong reputation in the data center, cloud, telecom, and energy industries.

Furthermore, HIVE has granted 1,090,000 RSUs to select employees, officers, directors, and consultants, including Mr. Tavares, under its RSU plan, subject to the TSX Venture Exchange's one-year minimum vesting period.

data.title: iQIYI Non-GAAP EPADS of -$0.01 in-line, revenue of $906M misses by $7.55M
data.description:
• iQIYI press release (NASDAQ:IQ): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of -$0.01 in-line.
  • Revenue of $906M (-14% Y/Y) misses by $7.55M.

  • Membership services revenue was $562.1 million, decreasing 15% year over year, primarily due to a lighter content slate.

  • Online advertising services revenue was $196.4 million, decreasing 13% year over year.

  • Content distribution revenue was $55.7 million, decreasing 20% year over year.

data.title: European markets slightly higher, led by defense stocks
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.01% to 8,766. UK's unemployment rate held steady at 4.4% from October to December 2024, lower than estimates of 4.5%.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.01% to 22,799

France (CAC:IND) -0.08% to 8,182. The inflation rate in France increased to 1.70% in January from 1.30% in December, above estimates of 1.4%.

Industrial production in Switzerland rose by 2.3% Y/Y in Q4.

The annual inflation rate in Sweden rose to 0.9% in January.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.04% higher as geopolitical tensions in the region boosted defense stocks, as European leaders called for higher defense spending at an emergency meeting in Paris. The Stoxx 600 Aerospace and Defense index, a gauge for the region's defense stocks, rose 4.6% to a record closing high on Monday.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area and Germany economic sentiment expected shortly.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 4 basis points to 4.51%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.50%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.56%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK).

data.title: Baidu Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.63 beats by $0.78, revenue of $4.67B beats by $70M
data.description:
• Baidu press release (NASDAQ:BIDU): Q4 Non-GAAP EPADS of $2.63 beats by $0.78.
  • Revenue of $4.67B (-2.0% Y/Y) beats by $70M.
  • Revenue from Baidu Core was RMB27.7 billion ($3.80 billion), increasing 1% year over year; online marketing revenue was RMB17.9 billion ($2.46 billion), decreasing 7% year over year, and non-online marketing revenue was RMB9.8 billion ($1.34 billion), up 18% year over year, mainly driven by AI Cloud business.
  • Revenue from iQIYI was RMB6.6 billion ($906 million), decreasing 14% year over year.
  • In December 2024, Baidu App's MAUs reached 679 million, up 2% year over year.
  • As of December 31, 2024, cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash and short-term investments were $19.06 billion, and cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash and short-term investments excluding iQIYI were $18.45 billion.
  • Free cash flow was $3 million, and free cash flow excluding iQIYI was negative $65 million.

data.title: OpenAI said to mull special voting rights to block hostile bids
data.description:
OpenAI is weighing special voting rights for its non-profit board to preserve its directors' decision-making power, sources told the Financial Times, a move that could help the ChatGPT maker fend off future hostile takeover attempts.

If granted, the special voting rights would allow the non-profit board to overrule major OpenAI investors like Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) and SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY).

The special voting rights are among new governance measures being evaluated by OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and board members as the company transitions to a for-profit structure.

OpenAI last week turned down a $97.4B takeover offer by consortium led by Elon Musk, who co-founded the company in 2015, but chose to leave in 2018.

"OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Musk's latest attempt to disrupt his competition," the company's chairman Bret Taylor had stated.
